docs.title: Airflow Azure Starter DAGs
docs.text:
2026-04-252026-04-26

Welcome — this docs site showcases example DAGs and starter code for running Airflow on Azure.

This repository serves as the DAG repository used for testing two related starter projects that demonstrate deploying Apache Airflow 3 on Azure:

  • Airflow Azure Starter (AKS)

  • Airflow Azure Starter (VM)

  • Repository: https://github.com/RaphCodec/airflow-azure-starter-dags

  • Docs engine: ProperDocs with the MaterialX theme

docs.title: Quick links
docs.text:
• Getting started: getting-started.md
  • DAG examples: look in the dags/ folder in the repo

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
2026-04-252026-04-26

Follow these steps to set up the repository and start developing with a devcontainer.

docs.title: Developing with a Dev Container (recommended)
docs.text:
Prerequisites

  • Install Docker Desktop and ensure Docker is running on your machine.
  • Install VS Code.

This repository includes configuration for a VS Code devcontainer so you can develop and preview docs in an isolated, reproducible environment.

  1. Clone the repo

    git clone https://github.com/RaphCodec/airflow-azure-starter-dags.git
cd airflow-azure-starter-dags

  2. Required VS Code extensions

    • Dev Containers (Microsoft) — provides the "Reopen in Container" command and container workspace features.
    • Docker (Microsoft) — useful for managing containers and images from VS Code.

  3. Open the repo in VS Code and start the devcontainer

    • Open the airflow-azure-starter-dags folder in VS Code.
    • Press Cmd+Shift+P (Ctrl+Shift+P on Windows/Linux) → Dev Containers: Reopen in Container.

The devcontainer will build and start; once it finishes building you can view the Airflow web UI at http://localhost:8080.

Notes:

  • Make sure Docker is running before reopening in the container; devcontainers require the Docker daemon.

docs.title: Running the Docs Development Server
docs.text:
2026-04-252026-04-26

The docs server is best run inside the dev container. The reason for this is that there are some pages that are automatically generated based on the Airflow DAGs. This automation would break outside of Linux/Unix.

To run the ProperDocs development server frm inside the container cd into the docs folder and then run the below command.

uv run properdocs serve -a 0.0.0.0:8000

View the development site as localhost:8000 in your web broswer.

docs.title: duckdb_example
docs.text:
2026-04-252026-04-26
  • DAG ID: duckdb_example
  • Description: A simple dag to show the use of a python virtual env with duckdb
  • Schedule: @daily
  • Catchup: False
  • Start date: 2025-08-01T05:00:00+00:00
  • Tags:

docs.title: Details
docs.text:
### Example Virtualenv DAG

This DAG demonstrates how to use the **`@task.virtualenv`** decorator in Airflow.
The task installs and runs a library (`duckdb`) **not included in Airflow or your base environment**.
Using `requirements` ensures the task runs in an isolated virtual environment
with the specified dependencies.

**Key points:**
- The `requirements` parameter pins the package version (`duckdb==1.1.1`).
- The virtual environment is created only for this task.
- Great for tasks needing custom dependencies without bloating the global Airflow image.
